SEARCH
Package com.cellsimulation.model

Class Person

java.lang.Object
com.cellsimulation.model.Person
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Person extends Object implements Serializable
An individual living on the simulation grid.

A Person owns a current health DiseaseState, a Position on the grid, a counter of how many ticks have been spent in the INFECTED state and an individual immunityFactor that linearly lowers its probability of being contaminated. It may also be vaccinated, in which case its effective immunity is raised to at least the configured vaccine efficacy. Its three behavioral methods (update(com.cellsimulation.model.SimulationSettings), move(com.cellsimulation.model.Grid, com.cellsimulation.neighborhood.NeighborhoodStrategy, com.cellsimulation.model.SimulationSettings) and spread(com.cellsimulation.model.Grid, com.cellsimulation.neighborhood.NeighborhoodStrategy, com.cellsimulation.model.SimulationSettings, int)) implement the SIR + Deceased disease propagation model:

  • update ages the infection and resolves death / natural recovery / forced recovery.
  • move optionally relocates the person to a free neighboring cell.
  • spread contaminates susceptible neighbors using a case-contact model.

The class is Serializable so that the whole simulation state can be persisted to disk through the SaveService.

See Also:

    Person(DiseaseState state, Position position)
    Creates a new Person with no individual immunity (immunityFactor == 0.0).

    Creates a new Person with the given initial state, position and immunity factor.

  • Constructor Details

    • Person

      public Person(DiseaseState state, Position position, double immunityFactor)
      Creates a new Person with the given initial state, position and immunity factor. The infection counter starts at zero.
      Parameters:
      state - the initial health state
      position - the initial position on the grid
      immunityFactor - the individual immunity factor in [0.0, 1.0]; it linearly lowers the probability of being contaminated

    • Person

      public Person(DiseaseState state, Position position)
      Creates a new Person with no individual immunity (immunityFactor == 0.0).

      Kept as a convenience so that call sites that do not deal with immunity keep compiling and behaving exactly as before this feature was introduced.

      Parameters:
      state - the initial health state
      position - the initial position on the grid

  • Method Details

    • getState

      public DiseaseState getState()
      Returns:
      the current health state of this person

    • getPosition

      public Position getPosition()
      Returns:
      the current position of this person on the grid

    • getInfectionDays

      public int getInfectionDays()
      Returns:
      the number of ticks elapsed since this person became infected

    • getImmunityFactor

      public double getImmunityFactor()
      Returns:
      the individual immunity factor in [0.0, 1.0]

    • setState

      public void setState(DiseaseState state)
      Sets the health state of this person.
      Parameters:
      state - the new state

    • setPosition

      public void setPosition(Position position)
      Sets the position of this person on the grid.
      Parameters:
      position - the new position

    • setImmunityFactor

      public void setImmunityFactor(double immunityFactor)
      Sets the individual immunity factor of this person.
      Parameters:
      immunityFactor - the new immunity factor in [0.0, 1.0]

    • isVaccinated

      public boolean isVaccinated()
      Returns:
      true if this person has been vaccinated

    • setVaccinated

      public void setVaccinated(boolean vaccinated)
      Sets the vaccination flag of this person.

      Vaccination is a permanent attribute: a vaccinated person that still gets infected (the vaccine is not necessarily perfect) keeps its vaccinated flag.

      Parameters:
      vaccinated - true to mark the person as vaccinated

    • isInfected

      public boolean isInfected()
      Returns:
      true if this person is currently infected

    • isDead

      public boolean isDead()
      Returns:
      true if this person has died from the disease

    • update

      public void update(SimulationSettings settings)
      Advances the disease for one tick.

      Only infected persons evolve. Each tick increments the infection counter and then resolves transitions in this strict order:

      1. mortality check (probability mortalityProbability);
      2. natural recovery (probability recoveryProbability);
      3. forced recovery once the counter reaches maxInfectionDays.
      Parameters:
      settings - the simulation settings supplying the probabilities and the infection duration cap

    • move

      public void move(Grid grid, NeighborhoodStrategy neighborhood, SimulationSettings settings)
      Optionally relocates this person to a random free neighboring cell.

      A deceased person never moves. Otherwise a single uniform draw decides whether the person attempts a move during this tick: the probability of attempting is mobilityRate. When the attempt succeeds and at least one neighboring cell is empty, a target is chosen uniformly at random among the empty neighbors and the grid is updated accordingly.

      Parameters:
      grid - the grid on which this person lives
      neighborhood - the neighborhood strategy used to identify the candidate cells
      settings - the simulation settings supplying the mobility rate

    • spread

      public void spread(Grid grid, NeighborhoodStrategy neighborhood, SimulationSettings settings, int currentTick)
      Spreads the disease to susceptible neighbors using a case-contact model.

      Only an infected person can transmit. For every neighbor returned by the strategy, a susceptible occupant becomes infected with an effective probability of transmissionProbability * (1 - effectiveImmunity). The neighbor's effective immunity is its individual immunityFactor, raised to the configured vaccineEfficacy when the neighbor is vaccinated. Empty cells and non-susceptible neighbors are ignored. This method never mutates this: only neighbors may change state.

      Parameters:
      grid - the grid on which this person lives
      neighborhood - the neighborhood strategy used to identify the candidate cells
      settings - the simulation settings supplying the transmission probability
      currentTick - the index of the tick being played; a person that became infected during this very tick does not propagate until the next one, which keeps the cellular-automaton update generational and the spread strictly limited to the chosen neighborhood
